info.name: ZapUnlocked API (auto-generated)
info.description: Auto-generated from OpenAPI. Base URL: http://localhost:8300

request.description:
Sends a text message to Meta AI via WhatsApp and waits for its response.

The message is forwarded to the Meta AI contact (13135550002@s.whatsapp.net). The server blocks until a reply is received or the `timeout` is reached.

Response includes:
- `text`: The AI's reply text
- `hasImage`: Whether the response includes an image
- `messageId`: The WhatsApp message ID of the reply

Requires an active WhatsApp connection.

Generates an image using Meta AI's /imagine feature.

Internally sends `/imagine {prompt}` to the Meta AI contact and returns the generated image along with any accompanying text.

Response shape same as /ai/ask with `hasImage: true` when successful.

Requires an active WhatsApp connection.
Get Collection.
Update Collection.

Get Contact Info.

Request body: `Contact` schema

Block User.

Request body: `Block` schema

Get Instance Device.

Return device data (Device, RawAgent, Integrator, etc).

Get Instance Me.

Return instance profile data (phone, jid, PushName, etc).
Updates the display name of this API instance. The name appears in logs and status responses.
Get Qr Page.
Get Qr Image.
Logout.

Pairs a new device via pairing code. This is an alternative to QR code scanning.

Required: `phone` (the phone number to pair with, including country code). The server will request a pairing code from WhatsApp.
Enables or disables automatic read receipts for incoming messages. When enabled, messages are automatically marked as read upon receipt.
Enables or disables automatic call rejection. When enabled, incoming calls are automatically rejected and the caller may see a configurable message.
Sets the message displayed when a call is automatically rejected. Only applies when `call-reject-auto` is enabled.

Get Phone Pair Code.

Path parameters: `phone`

Generate a pairing code to connect a number without QR Code.
Enter the code directly in WhatsApp (Linked Devices > Link with phone number).

Requires client to be awaiting connection (no active session).

Deletes a message for everyone (if possible) or for yourself.

Required: `phone`, `messageId`.

Edits an existing message. Works only for messages sent by the same bot.

Required: `messageId`, `message` (new text content).
Optional: `delay_message`, `delay_typing`, `mentioned`.

Marks a message (or multiple messages) as read.

Required: `messageIds` (array of message IDs).

body.raw:
{
    "phone": "{{PHONE}}",
    "callPhone": "+5511999999999",
    "button_text": "Call Now",
    "title": "Contact us",
    "text": "Call us now:",
    "delay_message": null,
    "delay_typing": null,
    "mentioned": null
}

request.description:
Sends a single call button message. The recipient sees a button that initiates a phone call when tapped.

Required: `callPhone`, `button_text`, `title`, `text`.
Optional: `delay_message`, `delay_typing`, `mentioned`.

Sends an interactive button list message. Supports up to 5 buttons of mixed types.

Button types available:
- `quick_reply`: Simple reply button with `buttonText` and `id`
- `url`: Opens a URL when tapped (`url` field)
- `call`: Initiates a phone call (`callPhone` field)
- `otp`: One-time password copy button (`code` field)

NOTE: PIX buttons are NOT supported in combined lists — use the dedicated /messages/send-button-pix endpoint instead.

Optional: `delay_message`, `delay_typing`, `mentioned`.

body.raw:
{
    "phone": "{{PHONE}}",
    "code": "591823",
    "button_text": "Copy Code",
    "title": "Security Verification",
    "text": "Your access code is: 591823",
    "footer": "Valid for 5 minutes.",
    "delay_typing": 1.5
}

request.description:
Sends a one-time password (OTP) button. The recipient can copy the code with one tap.

Required: `code`, `button_text`, `title`, `text`.
Optional: `footer` (string), `delay_message`, `delay_typing`, `mentioned`.

body.raw:
{
    "phone": "{{PHONE}}",
    "pixKey": "suachave@email.com",
    "pixType": "EMAIL",
    "pixValue": 19.9,
    "merchantName": "Minha Loja",
    "pixCity": "Sao Paulo",
    "pixDescription": "Compra #1234",
    "button_text": "Pagar",
    "title": "PIX Payment",
    "text": "Use the button below to pay via PIX:",
    "delay_message": null,
    "delay_typing": null,
    "mentioned": null
}

request.description:
Sends a PIX payment button (exclusive — cannot be combined with other button types).

Required:
- `pixKey`: The PIX key (email, phone, CPF, CNPJ, or random)
- `pixType`: Key type — EMAIL, PHONE, CPF, CNPJ, or RANDOM
- `pixValue`: Amount in BRL (float)
- `merchantName`: Your business/trading name
- `pixCity`: Your city

Optional: `pixDescription` (string), `button_text`, `title`, `text`, `delay_message`, `delay_typing`, `mentioned`.

Sends quick reply buttons (simple reply options shown as buttons).

Required: `title`, `text`, `buttons` (array of `{text, id}` objects).
Optional: `delay_message`, `delay_typing`, `mentioned`.

body.raw:
{
    "phone": "{{PHONE}}",
    "url": "https://zapunlocked.com",
    "button_text": "Visit Website",
    "title": "Get in touch",
    "text": "Visit our website:",
    "delay_message": null,
    "delay_typing": null,
    "mentioned": null
}

request.description:
Sends a single URL button message. The recipient sees a button that opens a URL when tapped.

Required: `url`, `button_text`, `title`, `text`.
Optional: `delay_message`, `delay_typing`, `mentioned`.

Sends an interactive list message with a menu of options. The user taps the button to expand the list and selects an option.

Required: `title`, `text`, `options` (array of option objects).
Optional: `button_text`, `footer`, `description`, `delay_message`, `delay_typing`, `mentioned`.

body.raw:
{
    "phone": "{{PHONE}}",
    "name": "What is your favorite color?",
    "options": [
        "Red",
        "Blue",
        "Green",
        "Yellow"
    ],
    "delay_message": null,
    "delay_typing": null,
    "mentioned": null
}

request.description:
Creates and sends a poll to a WhatsApp chat. Recipients can vote on options.

Required: `name` (poll question), `options` (array of strings, up to 12).
Optional: `delay_message`, `delay_typing`, `mentioned`.

Votes on an existing poll.

Required: `options` (array of selected option strings), `pollId` (the poll message ID).
Optional: `delay_message`, `delay_typing`, `mentioned`.

query.description: Filter by level: DEBUG, INFO, WARNING, ERROR, CRITICAL

request.description:
Returns server logs with powerful filtering. All parameters are optional query params.

Filters:
- `date` (YYYY-MM-DD): Specific day's logs (default: today)
- `from_date` / `to_date` (YYYY-MM-DD): Date range (alternative to `date`)
- `from_time` / `to_time` (HH:MM): Time range within the selected dates
- `level`: Filter by log level — DEBUG, INFO, WARNING, ERROR, CRITICAL
- `search`: Full-text search in log messages (case-insensitive)
- `limit`: Max lines to return (default: 200, max: 10000)
- `offset`: Skip first N matching lines (for pagination)

Use `date` OR (`from_date` + `to_date`), never both.

Response includes `filters` (echo of applied filters), `total`, `returned`, `offset`, and `logs` array.

Runs log maintenance: compresses old .log files to .gz, then deletes compressed files that exceed age (LOG_MAX_AGE_DAYS, default 30) or total size (LOG_MAX_SIZE_MB, default 50 MB) limits.

Response: `{"success": true, "compressed": N, "deleted": N}`

Lists all available log files with their date and size. Each log file covers one day.

Response: `{"success": true, "count": N, "files": [{"file": "...", "date": "YYYY-MM-DD", "sizeBytes": N, "sizeFormatted": "..."}]}`

Manual Cleanup.

Required: `interval` (cleanup interval in minutes). Older messages are purged periodically based on this setting.

Optional: `limit` (max messages to return, default 20).

Sends an audio message. Accepts URL or file upload.

Optional: `ptt` (boolean — send as voice note), `as_document` (boolean), `format` (string, e.g. "m4a", "ogg"), `delay_message`, `delay_typing`, `mentioned`.

Sends a document/file message. Accepts URL or file upload.

Optional: `fileName` (string — overrides the displayed filename), `caption` (string), `delay_message`, `delay_typing`, `mentioned`.

Sends an animated GIF. Accepts URL or file upload.

Optional: `caption` (string), `delay_message`, `delay_typing`, `mentioned`.

Sends an image message. Accepts either a URL or a file upload.

Use `url` (text field) to send from a remote URL, or `file` (file field) to upload from your local machine. If both are provided, `url` takes precedence.

Optional: `caption` (string), `as_document` (boolean — send as file instead of image), `delay_message`, `delay_typing`, `mentioned`.

Sends a sticker image. Accepts URL or file upload.

Additional sticker-specific fields:
- `pack`: Sticker pack name
- `author`: Sticker author
- `resize_mode`: 'pad' (add padding) or 'crop' (crop to fit)
- `pad_color`: Background color when using pad mode, e.g. 'black', 'white', '#FF0000'
- `blur_intensity`: Blur strength for the background padding (int)

Optional: `delay_message`, `delay_typing`, `mentioned`.

Sends a video message. Accepts URL or file upload.

Optional: `caption` (string), `as_document` (boolean), `delay_message`, `delay_typing`, `mentioned`.

Send Reaction.

Request body: `SendReaction` schema

body.raw:
{
    "phone": "{{PHONE}}",
    "message": "Hello! This is a test message.",
    "delay_message": null,
    "delay_typing": 2,
    "mentioned": null
}

request.description:
Sends a plain text message to a WhatsApp contact or group.

Optional parameters:
- `delay_message`: Server-side delay before sending. Can be a number (seconds) or a range string like "2-5" for random delay.
- `delay_typing`: Shows the "typing…" indicator for N seconds before sending. Simulates a human typing.
- `mentioned`: Array of phone numbers to @mention in group messages. Marked via ContextInfo — does NOT add @ symbol in text automatically. Add @ manually in `message` if you want visible mentions.
Enables or disables IP-based access control. When enabled, only whitelisted IPs can access the API. Requests from non-whitelisted IPs are blocked.
Enables or disables IP-based access control. When enabled, only whitelisted IPs can access the API. Requests from non-whitelisted IPs are blocked.

Get Ip Rules.

Return the current whitelist and blacklist.
Adds an IP address to the blacklist. Requires `ip-control` to be enabled.

Remove From Blacklist.

Path parameters: `ip`

Remove an IP address from the blacklist.
Adds an IP address to the whitelist. Requires `ip-control` to be enabled.

Remove From Whitelist.

Path parameters: `ip`

Remove an IP address from the whitelist.

Root Route.

Returns real-time runtime statistics including uptime (seconds + formatted), connection duration, message counters (sent/received), and webhook fire count. All counters reset on server restart.

Response shape:
`{"uptime": {"seconds": 3600, "formatted": "1h 0m 0s"}, "connected": {...}, "messages": {"sent": 42, "received": 128}, "webhooks": {"fired": 7}}`

Resets all runtime statistics counters (messages sent/received, webhooks fired) back to zero. Does NOT restart the server or disconnect WhatsApp.

Response: `{"success": true}`
Returns general connection and instance status. Use this endpoint to check whether the WhatsApp client is connected, the server is ready, and the instance name/ID.
Simple health-check endpoint. Returns HTTP 200 with a JSON body confirming the server is alive. Does not check WhatsApp connection status.
Readiness probe — returns HTTP 200 only when the server has completed its initialization (WhatsApp client ready). Useful for container orchestration health checks.
Returns current memory usage statistics: RSS, heap total, heap used, external, and array buffers. Useful for monitoring resource consumption.
Returns the current WhatsApp stream/connection state: connected, disconnected, connecting, etc.
Returns the volume (data usage) statistics for the current WhatsApp session. Includes sent/received byte counts.

Updates environment variables at runtime. Changes take effect immediately without server restart.

Creates a new webhook endpoint. The server will POST event data to this URL whenever matching events occur.

Required: `name`, `url` (your endpoint URL), `events` (array of event names or ["*"] for all events), `active` (boolean).

Available events:
- `message.*` — All message events
- `message.text`, `message.image`, `message.audio`, etc.
- `message.contacts` — Contact array messages
- `status.*` — Status changes
- `qr.*` — QR code events
- `call.*` — Call events
